steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The true subject is the presence (singular). Strip the long intervening modifier — "of many fossilized microbacteria, which seem to have thrived there despite the extreme heat that persisted after the Chicxulub impact" — and the skeleton reads: "the presence ______ claims that bacteria are among the planet's most resilient organisms." The verb must agree with a singular subject. Rule violated by wrong choices: subject-verb agreement.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'reinforce': Base plural form. "The presence reinforce" violates subject-verb agreement — singular subject requires the -s form. ✗
  • Choice B — 'have reinforced': Plural auxiliary "have." "The presence have reinforced" violates subject-verb agreement for the same reason. ✗
  • Choice C — 'reinforces': Singular third-person present (-s ending). "The presence reinforces claims" — subject and verb agree. ✓
  • Choice D — 'are reinforcing': Plural auxiliary "are." "The presence are reinforcing" violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
